title: Neomammillaria armillata
intro: Neomammillaria armillata, also known as the chain fruit cactus or armilla cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family Cactaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria armillata include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neomammillaria armillata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria armillata is classified as a species of cactus. It belongs to the genus Neomammillaria, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. The genus Neomammillaria is part of the family Cactaceae, which includes over 150 genera of cacti. The family Cactaceae is a part of the order Caryophyllales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Caryophyllales is within the class Magnoliopsida, which is one of the major divisions of flowering plants.
